Home / Travel Guide / Ferries & Hydrofoils

Travel Guide

Ferries & Hydrofoils

All maritime routes in Campania: Naples, Sorrento, Salerno, Positano, Amalfi, Capri, Ischia and Procida. Companies, indicative schedules and updated prices for the 2026 season.

All main routes

From To Duration Price (one way) Companies Seasonal
Naples Beverello Capri 40–50 min (hydrofoil) / 75 min (ferry) €20–23 / €13–15 NLG, Alilauro, SNAV, Caremar Year-round
Naples Beverello Ischia (Casamicciola/Porto) 50 min (hydrofoil) / 75 min (ferry) €20–24 / €12–14 Alilauro, SNAV, Caremar, Medmar Year-round
Naples Beverello Procida 35 min (hydrofoil) €15–18 Alilauro, SNAV Year-round
Sorrento Capri 20–25 min (hydrofoil) €18–22 NLG, Alilauro Year-round
Pozzuoli Ischia / Procida 35 min / 15 min (ferry) €10–13 / €7–9 Medmar, Caremar Year-round
Salerno Amalfi / Positano / Capri 45 min / 65 min / 100 min €8–12 / €12–16 / €18–22 Travelmar, NLG Apr–Oct
Naples Beverello Positano / Amalfi 75 min / 90 min €15–20 / €18–22 NLG, Alilauro Apr–Oct

* Prices and durations are indicative. Always verify with the companies: nlg.it, alilauro.it, snav.it, caremar.it, medmar.it, travelmar.it

Ferry companies

NLG

Naples→Capri, Sorrento→Capri. Fast and frequent hydrofoils. Book at nlg.it.

Alilauro

Naples→Capri/Ischia/Procida/Sorrento. The largest hydrofoil fleet. alilauro.it.

SNAV

Naples→Capri/Ischia/Procida. Also fast ferries. snav.it.

Caremar

Slow ferries Naples→Capri/Ischia/Procida. Cheapest but slowest. caremar.it.

Medmar

Ferries Pozzuoli→Ischia/Procida. Affordable and frequent. medmar.it.

Travelmar

Salerno→Amalfi→Positano seasonal route. travelmar.it.

Practical tips

  • In July and August, book tickets the day before: first morning sailings (7:00–8:30) sell out quickly.
  • Hydrofoils don't run in rough seas (Beaufort 4–5): always check marine weather forecasts.
  • The embarkation port in Naples for almost all routes is Molo Beverello (Piazza Municipio). Medmar departs from Pozzuoli.
  • With young children, the ferry is more stable than the hydrofoil: less rolling, accessible outdoor deck.
  • Return tickets can be purchased on the island - no need to buy the return leg upfront.

Frequently asked questions

What is the difference between a ferry and a hydrofoil on the Amalfi Coast?
The ferry (traghetto) is slower (50–90 min Naples–Capri) but cheaper and more stable. The hydrofoil (aliscafo) is faster (40–50 min) but more expensive and doesn't run in rough seas. In summer, prefer the hydrofoil to save time; in winter or bad weather, the ferry is more reliable.
Can I book Amalfi Coast ferries online?
Yes, almost all companies allow online booking: nlg.it, alilauro.it, snav.it, travelmar.it, caremar.it. In summer (July–August) it's recommended to book at least 1–2 days in advance for ferries from Naples to Capri and Ischia.
How much does the ferry from Naples to Capri cost?
Ferry Naples–Capri (Caremar/SNAV): ~€13–15 one way. Hydrofoil (NLG/Alilauro): ~€20–23 one way. From Sorrento: ~€18–22 return by hydrofoil. Prices vary slightly between companies and season.
Do ferries run in winter?
Yes, but with reduced frequency. Naples–Capri: at least 4–5 sailings per day year-round. Naples–Ischia: frequent year-round service. Seasonal routes (Positano, Amalfi, Salerno) run from April to October.

Amalfi Coast Travel helps travelers from around the world plan their trip to the Amalfi Coast through guides, travel tips, and much more. The entire project is lovingly curated by Alessia, directly from Massa Lubrense.

The Amalfi Coast - A UNESCO World Heritage Site
